programmes.show_description: Prepare to be blown away by performances from Opry member Steve Earle, Emmylou Harris, Marty Stuart and Vince Gill.
Coldplay's 2006 world tour arrives in Toronto, where they perform at the Air Canada Centre. The concert includes performances of hits such as Speed of Sound, Fix You and Talk.
Stereophonics: The Welsh rockers play and discuss Just Enough Education to Perform, their 2001 album which earned them huge global success, at The Clapham Grand. (S1, ep 2)
Carly Simon reveals the challenges behind No Secrets - the classic album that made her a global star and included the enigmatic song You're So Vain.
Alexander Armstrong and Myleene Klass present a concert of classical anthems at the Royal Albert Hall, with performances from Freddie De Tommaso, Hayato Sumino and more.
Art competition in which amateur watercolour painters compete against the clock and each other to capture a scene in just four hours. (S2, ep 15)
Art competition in which amateur watercolour painters compete against the clock and each other to capture a scene in just four hours. (S2, ep 16)
Mr Botibol's First Love: Jake Weston stars in this twisted story. A jaded businessman sells his company and attempts to change his life for the better by buying a strange piano. (S2, ep 8)
The Umbrella Man: John Mills stars in this story with a twist. A con man arrives in town and starts his scam. Michael Gambon guest stars. (S2, ep 9)
Fog Closing In: When her husband goes away on a business trip, Mary Summers is all alone in her house when an escaped patient breaks in. (S2, ep 2)
De Mortuis: Professor Rankin's buddies Wally and Bud stop by to take him fishing. Suspicions rise when they find him in his cellar, filling in a hole with cement. (S2, ep 3)
A look at the life of the inspirational filmmaker, who won the first-ever Academy Award for Writing Original Screenplay for his comedy film The Great McGinty. (S5, ep 7)
A look at the life of legendary two-time Academy Award winner Robert De Niro, star of The Godfather Part II, Taxi Driver and Goodfellas. (S10, ep 11)
The Flypaper: Lorna Charles and Alfred Burke star in this twisted tale. With a 12 year-old found dead and a killer perhaps on the loose, a young girl becomes paranoid. (S2, ep 10)
The Orderly World of Mr Appleby: Robert Lang stars in this tale with a twist. A man married three times might be lucky for the fourth time when he meets a new interest. (S2, ep 11)

programmes.show_name: Wonderland: From JM Barrie. To JRR Tolkien
programmes.show_description: In a four-part series, immerse yourself in the worlds of some of history's finest authors, revealing much about their character and the time they lived in. (1 of 4)

programmes.show_name: Stories From The National Portrait Gallery
programmes.show_description: With the National Portrait Gallery reopening, Kate Bryan discusses the refurbishment with its director and begins a journey of portraiture through the ages. (S1, ep 1)
A look at the life and career of the Hollywood filmmaker, famous for directing films such as the Academy Award-nominated Blossoms in the Dust and Thirty Seconds Over Tokyo. (S5, ep 8)
A glimpse into the life of French star and winner of the 2019 Honorary Palme d'Or, Alain Delon. (S10, ep 12)
Fat Chance: A pharmacist tries to elope with his lover, but his larger than life wife stands in his way. John Castle and Miriam Margolyes star in this tale with a twist. (S2, ep 12)
Back For Christmas: A philandering physician with an obsession for orchids cultivates a deadly plot to see off his wife. Siân Phillips and Richard Johnson star. (S3, ep 1)
Kill with Kindness: Katherine Oldham feeds stew to a homeless man as she struggles to keep the mind of her brother, Fitzhugh on the task of murder. (S2, ep 4)
None Are So Blind: An antiques dealer feels he should be enjoying the finer things in life, but his Aunt Muriel is the one with all the money. (S2, ep 5)
...Concert Live In Amsterdam. Andre Rieu performs songs, including a specially-penned Coronation Waltz, at the investiture of King Willem Alexander and Queen Maxima on 30 April 2013.

programmes.show_name: Andre Rieu: Welcome To My World
programmes.show_description: André Rieu: Falling in Love Again: André performs his emotional first concert after the pandemic and welcomes a new soprano to the Johann Strauss family. (S5, ep 2)
Barry Cryer looks back on the life and career of the Academy Award-nominee comedian and actress who starred in Paper Moon and Blazing Saddles. (S2, ep 4)

programmes.show_name: Peter O'Toole: Along The Sky Road to Aqaba
programmes.show_description: Feature documentary reflecting on Peter O'Toole's towering theatrical legacy. Includes interviews with Sir Anthony Hopkins, Sir Derek Jacobi and Stephen Fry.

programmes.show_name: The World In A Painting: Caravaggio's Comb
programmes.show_description: With details from Caravaggio's painting, explore the history of ivory trade routes from Africa and the Arctic to 16th-century Europe.
...Great Museums Of The World. A walk through some of the most remarkable museums ever, including Spain's Museum of Arts and Sciences and the Swiss Museum designed by Renzo Piano.
New. David Hockney's Portrait of an Artist (Pool with Two Figures) is a modern masterpiece and now it's up for sale at Christie's in New York with an estimate of $80 million.
A work by Le Corbusier joins a host of Old Masters and modern British and Irish paintings up for auction at Bonhams. (S10, ep 8)
Married to Laurence Olivier, the actress Vivien Leigh had star quality and Sotheby's are hoping her name will attract bidders to a two-day sale of her possessions. (S10, ep 9)
In a captivating voyage that captures the very essence of adventure, this story of transformation unfolds through a combination of acrobatics in a visually striking world.

This evocative and unique film features the only TV interview ever given by Laurie Lee, including the amazing story of how it was secured by local TV producer David Parker.
Melencolia I, by Albrecht Durer: Created in 1514 and widely regarded as the greatest print ever made, Durer's Melencolia has defied all attempts to understand it. (S3, ep 1)
Fountain by Marcel Duchamp: Voted the most influential artwork of the 20th century, Marcel Duchamp's upturned urinal changed the course of art - creating 'conceptual art'. (S3, ep 2)
A look at the life of the Academy Award-winning director whose work includes Terms of Endearment and multi Emmy Award-winning sitcom The Simpsons. (S5, ep 9)
Take a look at the life and career of Academy Award-winning Dame Maggie Smith as we mark the sad passing of one of the UK's most cherished acting greats. (S10, ep 13)
Picture Of A Place: Hazel is an elderly widow who lives in a farmhouse full of antiques. When a conman arrives he dupes her into selling him a picture at a knock-down price. (S3, ep 2)
Proof Of Guilt: George is denying murder, even though he was in a locked room with the victim when he was shot. Inspector Walters just can't find any evidence against him. (S3, ep 3)
Toby: In early 20th century New York, bookkeeper Al Birch expects the arrival of his sweetheart Edwina from 20 years ago. (S2, ep 6)
Alibi Me: Georgie Minnelli kills his old childhood enemy Lucky Moore for interfering in his pinball machine racket. Now Georgie desperately needs an alibi. (S2, ep 7)

programmes.show_name: The Michelangelo Code: Secrets of the Sistine Chapel
programmes.show_description: Film following art critic Waldemar Januszczak, who sets out to prove that Michelangelo's greatest work contains a veiled meaning.

programmes.show_name: Gene Kelly: An American In Hollywood
programmes.show_description: From his beginnings in cabarets and on Broadway to his recognition as a choreographer and director, the star with a charming smile expanded the boundaries of dance expression.
Jake Chapman explores a mind-bending vision of the world that few can see. Are we, capitalism and technology already one and the same - accelerating towards an inevitable climax?
